Name: gpu_memory_bandwidth_optimizer
Title: Optimizing GPU Memory Bandwidth for Deep Learning Workloads with Novel Kernel Fusion Techniques and Autotuned Tensor Layouts
Short Hypothesis: By leveraging novel kernel fusion techniques and autotuned tensor layouts, we can significantly optimize GPU memory bandwidth for deep learning workloads, leading to improved performance and reduced latency.
Related Work: Existing works such as AMP-ViT, SNIP, and EMQ have focused on optimizing vision transformers, large language models, and mixed-precision quantization, respectively. However, these works do not directly address the optimization of GPU memory bandwidth. Our proposal builds upon these works by exploring novel kernel fusion techniques and autotuned tensor layouts to optimize memory bandwidth, which is a critical component of deep learning workloads.
Abstract: Deep learning workloads are increasingly dominated by memory-bound operations, making GPU memory bandwidth a critical bottleneck. This proposal explores novel kernel fusion techniques and autotuned tensor layouts to optimize GPU memory bandwidth for deep learning workloads. We propose to develop a framework that automatically fuses kernels and optimizes tensor layouts to minimize memory access patterns and maximize memory bandwidth utilization. Our approach will be evaluated on a range of deep learning benchmarks, including vision transformers and large language models, and will be compared to state-of-the-art baselines. We expect our approach to achieve significant improvements in performance and reduced latency, making it an attractive solution for real-world deep learning applications.
Experiments: Evaluate the performance of our kernel fusion technique on a range of deep learning benchmarks, including vision transformers and large language models.
Compare the performance of our autotuned tensor layouts to existing layouts, such as row-major and column-major layouts.
Investigate the impact of memory bandwidth optimization on the overall performance of deep learning workloads, including latency and throughput.
Conduct ablation studies to identify the most critical components of our approach and quantify their contributions to the overall performance improvements.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of deep learning workloads and the variability of GPU architectures may limit the applicability and effectiveness of our approach.
The optimization of kernel fusion and tensor layouts may require significant computational resources and expertise, which could be a barrier to adoption.
The evaluation of our approach may be sensitive to the specific benchmarks and hardware used, which could impact the generality of our results.
